disclaimer: Informational only — not legal or tax advice. Verify requirements with official state and IRS sources before filing.

california.name: California
california.contractorTest: ABC test — worker is independent only if free from control, work is outside usual course of business, and worker has an independent trade
california.notes1099: California does not require separate state 1099-NEC copies for most payers, but you must still file federal Form 1099-NEC when payments reach $600. FTB may receive federal data via IRS matching. Keep W-9s on file before first payment.
california.notesLLC: Form LLC-1 online via bizfileonline.sos.ca.gov. Every LLC owes at least $800 annual franchise tax even with zero income. Single-member LLCs default to disregarded entity for federal tax unless Form 8832 is filed.
california.notesContractor: Collect signed W-9 before paying any contractor. Misclassification penalties under AB 5 are severe — document scope, deadlines, and deliverables. General liability insurance is commonly required by clients even when not mandated by state law.
california.notesTax: California has progressive state income tax (1%–13.3%). Freelancers owe estimated tax to FTB on the same quarterly schedule as IRS. Jan 31 deadline to furnish 1099-NEC copies to recipients and file with IRS.

texas.name: Texas
texas.contractorTest: Common-law control test — IRS and Texas Workforce Commission look at behavioral, financial, and relationship factors
texas.notes1099: Texas has no state income tax, so there is no state 1099 income reporting form. Federal 1099-NEC rules still apply at $600+. Sales tax on services varies by activity — check Comptroller guidance if you sell taxable services.
texas.notesLLC: File Certificate of Formation (Form 205) with the Secretary of State. No annual franchise tax report due until revenue exceeds $2.47M (2026 threshold — verify current year). Operating agreement recommended though not filed with the state.
texas.notesContractor: Texas does not require workers comp for most independent contractors, but clients often require COI. Non-compete enforceability is limited under Texas Business & Commerce Code. Always obtain W-9 before first payment.
texas.notesTax: No state income tax on freelance earnings, but self-employment tax (15.3%) still applies federally. Estimated federal payments due quarterly. Jan 31: deliver 1099-NEC to contractors and file with IRS.

florida.name: Florida
florida.contractorTest: Right-to-control test aligned with IRS common-law factors; construction trades have additional licensing via DBPR
florida.notes1099: Florida has no personal income tax and no state 1099 filing requirement. Federal Form 1099-NEC still required at $600+ for nonemployee compensation paid to U.S. persons.
florida.notesLLC: File Articles of Organization via Sunbiz. Annual report due May 1 each year ($138.75 fee). Single-member LLCs are disregarded entities by default for federal tax.
florida.notesContractor: Construction contractors need DBPR licensing; most other freelancers do not. Collect W-9 and proof of insurance when clients require it. Reemployment tax applies to employees, not properly classified ICs.
florida.notesTax: No Florida income tax on freelance income. Federal estimated tax still required. Sales tax may apply to certain professional services — check DOR nexus rules. Jan 31 federal 1099-NEC deadline unchanged.

new-york.name: New York
new-york.dorName: New York State Department of Taxation and Finance
new-york.llcFormationFee: $200 filing + publication requirement ($300–$1,500+ in NYC counties)
new-york.contractorTest: ABC test for unemployment purposes; IRS common-law test for federal classification
new-york.notes1099: New York requires payers to file Form 1099-NEC/MISC information with NYS when federal filing is required. Use NYS Tax Department online services. NYC residents may owe additional NYC income tax.
new-york.notesLLC: File Articles of Organization with DOS. LLC publication requirement in two newspapers within 120 days — costly in NYC. Biennial statement fee $9 every two years. Consider LLC vs sole prop carefully given publication costs.
new-york.notesContractor: NY treats worker misclassification aggressively. Construction industry has strict IC rules. Maintain written contracts, W-9, and proof of separate business entity. Disability benefits may apply to employees only.
new-york.notesTax: NY state income tax 4%–10.9% plus NYC local tax if applicable. Estimated tax to NYS on same dates as IRS. Jan 31: federal 1099-NEC to recipients; NYS information returns typically due by last day of February.

illinois.name: Illinois
illinois.contractorTest: ABC test for unemployment; IRS 20-factor common-law test for federal
illinois.notes1099: Illinois requires Form IL-1099 when federal 1099 reporting is required for Illinois residents or payments sourced to Illinois. File through MyTax Illinois. Keep records for 3 years minimum.
illinois.notesLLC: File Articles of Organization with Secretary of State. Annual report $75 due before first day of anniversary month. Personal property replacement tax may apply to LLCs with income.
illinois.notesContractor: Construction contractors must register with IDFPR. Collect W-9 before payment. Workers comp required for employees; ICs should carry their own GL insurance. Written scope-of-work reduces audit risk.
illinois.notesTax: Illinois flat 4.95% individual income tax on freelance earnings. Estimated payments to IDOR quarterly. Jan 31 federal 1099-NEC deadline; IL-1099 typically due by February 28.

pennsylvania.name: Pennsylvania
pennsylvania.contractorTest: ABC test for UC purposes; three-factor IRS test (behavioral, financial, type of relationship)
pennsylvania.notes1099: Pennsylvania requires REV-1667 information returns when federal 1099s are filed for PA payees. Philadelphia has separate business tax rules — check BIRT if operating in Philly.
pennsylvania.notesLLC: Register via PA Business One-Stop Hub. Decennial report required every 10 years ($70). Foreign LLCs doing business in PA must register. Operating agreement strongly recommended.
pennsylvania.notesContractor: Home improvement contractors need HIC registration ($50). Always collect W-9. PA treats misclassified workers as employees for UC and withholding. Maintain invoices showing project-based work.
pennsylvania.notesTax: PA flat 3.07% personal income tax plus local earned income tax (EIT) in most municipalities. Estimated payments quarterly to PA DOR. Jan 31 federal 1099-NEC; PA REV-1667 due per DOR schedule.

ohio.name: Ohio
ohio.contractorTest: IRS common-law test; Ohio RC 4141 for unemployment classification
ohio.notes1099: Ohio does not require a separate state 1099 form for most payers. Federal 1099-NEC filing at $600+ still mandatory. Municipal income tax (RITA/CCA) may apply in many cities — register where you work.
ohio.notesLLC: File Articles of Organization with Secretary of State. No annual report fee for domestic LLCs. Commercial activity tax (CAT) applies when gross receipts exceed $150,000.
ohio.notesContractor: Collect W-9 before first payment. Some trades require Ohio contractor licensing. Maintain written contracts specifying deliverables, not hours. GL insurance commonly required by corporate clients.
ohio.notesTax: Ohio progressive state tax (0%–3.5% on first $26,050 for 2026 — verify brackets). City taxes add 1%–3% in many areas. Estimated payments to ODT and municipal collectors. Jan 31 federal 1099-NEC deadline.

georgia.name: Georgia
georgia.contractorTest: ABC test for unemployment; IRS common-law for federal withholding
georgia.notes1099: Georgia requires G-2A annual information return summarizing 1099 data when federal forms are filed for GA recipients. Access via Georgia Tax Center (GTC).
georgia.notesLLC: File Articles of Organization via eCorp. Annual registration $50 due April 1 each year. Single-member LLCs file Schedule C unless electing corporate treatment.
georgia.notesContractor: Residential and general contractors need Secretary of State licensing. Collect W-9 and COI. Georgia treats IC misclassification as wage theft in some cases — document independence clearly.
georgia.notesTax: Georgia flat 5.49% income tax (2026 rate — verify). Estimated payments via GTC quarterly. Jan 31 federal 1099-NEC; G-2A annual summary typically due end of February.

north-carolina.name: North Carolina
north-carolina.contractorTest: Common-law control test; NC ESC uses ABC test for unemployment
north-carolina.notes1099: North Carolina requires NC-1099 information returns when federal 1099s are filed for NC payees. File through NCDOR eServices. Withholding may apply to certain non-wage compensation.
north-carolina.notesLLC: File Articles of Organization with SOS. Annual report $200 due by April 15. Franchise tax may apply to LLCs taxed as corporations.
north-carolina.notesContractor: General contractors need NC Licensing Board registration for projects over $30,000. Collect W-9 before payment. Workers comp required for employees; ICs should carry own policy.
north-carolina.notesTax: NC flat 4.25% individual income tax (2026 — verify). Estimated payments to NCDOR quarterly. Jan 31 federal 1099-NEC; NC-1099 due per NCDOR calendar.

michigan.name: Michigan
michigan.contractorTest: IRS 20-factor test; Michigan UIA uses economic reality test
michigan.notes1099: Michigan does not require separate state 1099 copies for most payers. Federal 1099-NEC at $600+ still required. City income tax applies in Detroit, Grand Rapids, and other municipalities.
michigan.notesLLC: File Articles of Organization with LARA. Annual statement $25 due by February 15. Single-member LLCs default to disregarded entity status federally.
michigan.notesContractor: Residential builders need M&A license from LARA. Collect W-9 and maintain 1099 tracking spreadsheet. Misclassification triggers UIA back assessments plus penalties.
michigan.notesTax: Michigan flat 4.25% income tax. City taxes add 1%–2.4% where applicable. Estimated payments via Michigan Treasury Online quarterly. Jan 31 federal 1099-NEC deadline.

new-jersey.name: New Jersey
new-jersey.contractorTest: ABC test for unemployment; IRS common-law for federal withholding
new-jersey.notes1099: New Jersey requires NJ-W-3M annual reconciliation when federal 1099s are issued to NJ recipients. File through NJ Division of Taxation. NJ-1099 may be required for certain compensation types.
new-jersey.notesLLC: File Certificate of Formation via NJ Business Formation portal. Annual report $75 due end of anniversary month. Single-member LLCs default to disregarded entity unless Form 8832 elected.
new-jersey.notesContractor: Home improvement contractors need NJ Consumer Affairs registration. Collect W-9 before first payment. Worker misclassification triggers DOL penalties and back unemployment premiums.
new-jersey.notesTax: NJ progressive income tax 1.4%–10.75%. Estimated payments via NJ-1040-ES quarterly. Jan 31 federal 1099-NEC; NJ-W-3M reconciliation per NJ Division of Taxation calendar.

virginia.name: Virginia
virginia.contractorTest: IRS 20-factor test; Virginia Employment Commission uses right-to-control test
virginia.notes1099: Virginia does not require separate state 1099 copies for most payers. Federal 1099-NEC at $600+ still required. VA-6 annual withholding reconciliation may apply if backup withholding was taken.
virginia.notesLLC: File Articles of Organization via SCC eFile. Annual registration fee $50 due by end of LLC anniversary month. Pass-through taxation default for single-member LLCs.
virginia.notesContractor: Class A/B/C contractor licenses required for projects over $1,000 via DPOR. Collect W-9 and COI. Misclassification audits focus on exclusive full-time IC arrangements.
virginia.notesTax: Virginia flat 5.75% income tax (verify current rate). Estimated payments via VA-760ES quarterly. Jan 31 federal 1099-NEC deadline.

washington.name: Washington
washington.contractorTest: Economic dependence test for workers comp; IRS common-law for federal tax
washington.notes1099: Washington has no state income tax. Federal 1099-NEC rules apply at $600+. B&O tax may apply to gross receipts from services — verify with Department of Revenue.
washington.notesLLC: File Certificate of Formation via SOS Corporations Filing System. Annual report $60 due by end of anniversary month. No state income tax on LLC profits.
washington.notesContractor: General contractors register with L&I. Independent contractors must meet RCW 51.08.195 criteria to avoid workers comp premiums. Collect W-9 before payment.
washington.notesTax: No state income tax. Self-employment tax (15.3%) still applies federally. B&O tax on business gross income may apply. Jan 31 federal 1099-NEC deadline.

arizona.name: Arizona
arizona.contractorTest: IRS common-law control test; Arizona DES uses ABC test for unemployment
arizona.notes1099: Arizona does not require separate state 1099 copies for most payers. Federal 1099-NEC at $600+ still required. Transaction privilege tax may apply to certain services.
arizona.notesLLC: File Articles of Organization via ACC eCorp. No annual report fee for LLCs (verify current ACC fee schedule). Single-member LLCs taxed as disregarded entities by default.
arizona.notesContractor: ROC license required for residential and commercial contractors. Collect W-9 and verify license number. IC misclassification triggers DES back premiums.
arizona.notesTax: Arizona flat 2.5% income tax (2026 — verify). Estimated payments via Form 140ES quarterly. Jan 31 federal 1099-NEC deadline.

massachusetts.name: Massachusetts
massachusetts.contractorTest: ABC test under MGL c. 149 § 148B; strict independent contractor rules
massachusetts.notes1099: Massachusetts requires Form M-1099-K or equivalent information returns for certain payments to MA residents. DOR receives federal 1099 data via IRS matching. Verify current M-1099 thresholds with DOR.
massachusetts.notesLLC: File Certificate of Organization with Secretary of Commonwealth. Annual report $500 due each year. LLCs taxed as pass-through unless corporate election filed.
massachusetts.notesContractor: MA has strict ABC test — most workers are employees unless all three prongs met. Construction supervisors need CS license. Always collect W-9; document project-based scope.
massachusetts.notesTax: MA flat 5% income tax plus 4% surtax on income over $1M. Estimated payments via Form 1-ES quarterly. Jan 31 federal 1099-NEC; state forms per DOR calendar.

tennessee.name: Tennessee
tennessee.llcFormationFee: $300 minimum filing fee (varies by member count)
tennessee.contractorTest: IRS common-law test; Tennessee uses ABC test for unemployment
tennessee.notes1099: Tennessee has no state income tax on wages or self-employment. Federal 1099-NEC at $600+ still required. Business tax (BTO) may apply based on gross receipts.
tennessee.notesLLC: File Articles of Organization via SOS online. Annual report $50 per member (minimum $300, maximum $3,000). Franchise and excise tax may apply to LLCs with default classification.
tennessee.notesContractor: Contractor licenses required via TN Board for Licensing Contractors for projects over $25,000. Collect W-9 before payment. Workers comp required for employees.
tennessee.notesTax: No state income tax on freelance earnings. Federal self-employment tax (15.3%) still applies. Franchise/excise tax may apply to LLCs — consult CPA. Jan 31 federal 1099-NEC deadline.

indiana.name: Indiana
indiana.contractorTest: IRS common-law test; Indiana DWD uses ABC test for unemployment
indiana.notes1099: Indiana does not require separate state 1099 copies for most payers. Federal 1099-NEC at $600+ still required. County income tax may apply in addition to state rate.
indiana.notesLLC: File Articles of Organization via INBiz. Business entity report due every 2 years ($31 online). Single-member LLCs default to disregarded entity status.
indiana.notesContractor: Contractors need license from IPLA for projects over certain thresholds. Collect W-9 and verify insurance. Misclassification triggers DWD audits.
indiana.notesTax: Indiana flat 3.05% state tax plus county surtax (0.5%–3.38%). Estimated payments via ES-40 quarterly. Jan 31 federal 1099-NEC deadline.

missouri.name: Missouri
missouri.contractorTest: IRS common-law test; Missouri uses ABC test for unemployment
missouri.notes1099: Missouri requires Form MO-1099 when federal 1099-NEC or 1099-MISC is filed for MO recipients. File through MyTax Missouri. Direct state filing required — do not assume IRS forwarding covers MO.
missouri.notesLLC: File Articles of Organization via SOS online. No annual report fee for LLCs. Single-member LLCs taxed as disregarded entities by default.
missouri.notesContractor: Contractors may need local licensing depending on municipality. Collect W-9 before first payment. MO-1099 filing mandatory when federal 1099s issued to MO payees.
missouri.notesTax: Missouri progressive income tax up to 4.8% (2026 — verify). Estimated payments via MO-1040ES quarterly. Jan 31 federal 1099-NEC; MO-1099 per DOR calendar.

maryland.name: Maryland
maryland.contractorTest: IRS common-law test; Maryland DLLR uses ABC test for unemployment
maryland.notes1099: Maryland requires MW508 annual information return reconciling 1099 data for MD recipients. File through bFile Maryland. State copies may be required for certain payment types.
maryland.notesLLC: File Articles of Organization via Maryland Business Express. Annual report and personal property return due April 15 each year ($300 minimum franchise tax for LLCs).
maryland.notesContractor: Home improvement contractors need MHIC license. Collect W-9 and COI. MW508 reconciliation due when federal 1099s filed for MD payees.
maryland.notesTax: Maryland progressive income tax 2%–5.75% plus county rates. Estimated payments via Form PV quarterly. Jan 31 federal 1099-NEC; MW508 per Comptroller calendar.

wisconsin.name: Wisconsin
wisconsin.contractorTest: IRS common-law test; Wisconsin DWD uses economic reality test
wisconsin.notes1099: Wisconsin does not require separate state 1099 copies for most payers. Federal 1099-NEC at $600+ still required. Form 1099-K reporting may apply for payment processors.
wisconsin.notesLLC: File Articles of Organization via DFI online. Annual report $25 due by end of quarter containing anniversary date. Single-member LLCs default to disregarded entity.
wisconsin.notesContractor: Dwelling contractor certification required for residential work via DSPS. Collect W-9 before payment. Worker misclassification triggers DWD back assessments.
wisconsin.notesTax: Wisconsin progressive income tax 3.5%–7.65%. Estimated payments via Form 1-ES quarterly. Jan 31 federal 1099-NEC deadline.
